Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Prayer For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Apr-21-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TINA W. LU OF THE LAW OFFICES OF JOHN A BIARD, TEL. NO. (925) 945-4003 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LARK CREEK CAFE, INC.'S Motion To Strike Prayer For Punitive Damages - The motion to strike the claim for punitive damages is granted without leave to amend. Under Civil Code section 3294, malice is defined as either (1) conduct which is intended by the defendant to cause injury to the plaintiff or (2) "despicable conduct which is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others." (Italics added.) " 'Despicable conduct' is conduct which is so vile, base, contemptible, miserable, wretched or loathsome that it would be looked down upon and despised by ordinary decent people." Mock v. Michigan Millers Mutual Ins. Co. (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 306, 331. Here, the complaint alleges conduct that sounds in negligence, not "despicable" conduct. Plaintiff's allegations that defendant acted intentionally are conclusory. The court finds McDonnell v. American Trust Co. (1955) 130 Cal.App.2d 296, 300, to be persuasive. That case did not allow punitive damages in similar circumstances and the decision analyzed a version of CC 3294 that allowed the recovery of punitive damages in a more liberal manner than the present version of the statute. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR # 12913
